title: Spectrophotometric Study of the Reaction of Chromium(Vl) with Fluphenazine Hydrochloride
author: H. SANKE GOWDA
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Post-graduate Studies and Research in Chemistry, Manasagangotri, Mysore-570 006 

 Fluphenazine hydrochloride forms a red coloured species with chromium(VI) instantaneously at room temperature in 2-5.5  M  phosphoric acid medium. A 29-fold molar excess of the reagent is necessary for maximum development of colour intensity. The red coloured species exhibits maximum absorbance at 500 nm with molar absorptivity of 2.616 x 10 4  litre mol -1  cm -1  . Beer's law is valid over the concentration range of 0.05-1.85 ppm of chromium(VI). The method offers the advantages of simplicity, rapidity and sensitivity without the need for heating or extraction.

The method offers the advantages of simplicity, rapidity and sensitivity without the need for heating or extraction.
